text.title: ai-assisted-accessibility Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-assisted-accessibility as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using AI overlay widgets (UserWay, accessiBe) as an accessibility compliance solution.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Overlays do not fix underlying code issues — they are band-aids that cannot fix semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, heading structure, or meaningful alt text. They may create new barriers for screen reader users and do not satisfy WCAG 2 compliance.

failure-mode.description: Accepting AI-generated alt text without editorial review for context, brand voice, and image purpose.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI describes what is in the image; it cannot determine why the image matters to the user, what should be emphasized, or the appropriate level of detail for the context. Always include surrounding page context in the generation prompt.

failure-mode.description: Publishing video with AI-only captions without human review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI-only caption accuracy (80–90%) does not meet the 99% legal requirement. Always pair AI captioning with human review before publication. Legal requirement is non-negotiable.

failure-mode.description: Using AI scan results alone as proof of WCAG compliance for legal or procurement purposes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Even AI-enhanced scanning covers only 30–50% of WCAG success criteria. The remaining criteria require human testing and judgment. Presenting automated results as compliance evidence is legally and ethically inaccurate.

failure-mode.description: Sending large axe JSON output (100+ issues) to LLM in a single prompt without chunking.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Large axe reports on poorly maintained pages exceed LLM context limits. Chunk by page or component before sending; aggregate results after.

failure-mode.description: Agent instructed to suggest overlays or widget-based fixes as remediation options.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Overlays are never an acceptable remediation. The agent must be explicitly instructed to skip overlay suggestions — without the constraint, it will recommend them as quick fixes.
